generated_by: Generated by ops/scripts/compile-content.mjs. Do not edit by hand.

interviews.title: Why World War 3 Is Becoming a Structural Fight
interviews.source_title: Professor Jiang: World War 3 Is About To Begin, Let Me Explain!
interviews.dek: Jiang treats the Middle East conflict and global monetary system as parts of one strategic architecture: empire, geography, and control of energy channels.

interviews.title: "Heading For A Global DEPRESSION!"
interviews.source_title: \"Heading For A Global DEPRESSION!\" Professor Jiang Debates Iran War, Trump And China Ties
interviews.dek: Jiang reframes Hormuz disruption as a production-system collapse and argues that escalation incentives make the Iran conflict a political-economic choke point beyond price shocks.

interviews.title: The War Continues, So Financial Power Keeps Its Levers
interviews.source_title: 🔴 Prof Jiang & Alex Krainer - The Elites Exit Plan REVEALED
interviews.dek: Jiang frames the Iran conflict as a managed long war: visible ceasefires do not remove structural incentives that keep military pressure, debt extraction, and elite coordination in place.

interviews.title: Iran as Recalibration, Not Exit
interviews.source_title: Prof. Jiang: Iran Has Trump TRAPPED! Full Interview | Redacted w Clayton Morris
interviews.dek: Jiang treats the Iran shock as a long-cycle pressure system: initial strikes fail, the state shifts to durable economic coercion, and public attention is expected to absorb scarcity, distraction, and control mechanisms as this drags on.

interviews.title: The Final Days of the U.S. Empire
interviews.source_title: The Final Days Of The U.S. Empire! – Full Interview w/ Professor Jiang
interviews.dek: The interview frames U.S. action in Iran as a strategic shift toward prolonged strategic pressure rather than a clean ceasefire, then extends the pattern to bureaucratic overreach, surveillance infrastructure, and social fragmentation while proposing organized labor as the practical counterweight.

interviews.title: Why This Iran War Feels Like a Bear Trap
interviews.source_title: Is the Iran War ACTUALLY Over? w/ Professor Jiang: LIVE 6 pm PST
interviews.dek: Jiang frames the Iran war as a structural problem: empires that enter forceful conflicts without strategic reserve burn out, and the current administration is trying to steer around collapse, domestic optics, and a volatile coalition.

interviews.title: The Strait Is the Nuclear Weapon
interviews.source_title: “It's An Act Of WAR!” Professor Jiang vs Gordon Chang On China, Iran & Trump | Plus Robert Pape
interviews.dek: The interview begins as a fight over whether the Iran war has helped anyone, then turns into a harder question: what happens when a regional war reveals that waterways, energy corridors, diaspora hopes, and presidential threats are all weaker than the physical chokepoint underneath them?

interviews.title: The World Runs on Borrowed Time
interviews.source_title: 🔴 Jiang Xueqin Warns: The End of The World Has Begun (Here's Why)
interviews.dek: The interview starts with a ceasefire question and ends in a resource apocalypse. Jiang's answer is that the Iran war is no longer only about Iran: Hormuz, Malacca, Venezuela, the Levant, NATO, China, and the American home front are all pieces of a world where empire is moving from consent to force.

interviews.title: Empire Is Evil, but It Pays
interviews.source_title: SNEAKO X Professor Jiang X Dave Smith | Unity Amidst Chaos - Full Panel Discussion
interviews.dek: Dave asks Jiang what he is missing. Jiang's answer is that the Iran war cannot be explained only by Trump, Netanyahu, or the Israel lobby. Those actors matter, but the deeper machine is imperial decline: an empire whose beneficiaries know it is evil, know it pays, and cannot make the sacrifices needed to leave it.

interviews.title: Every Technology Needs a Front Man
interviews.source_title: \"They Created Bitcoin!\" Professor Jiang Exposes Why Every Technology Needs A \"Front Man\"│Jack Neel
interviews.dek: The interview starts with the end of the world and Satoshi Nakamoto, but the deeper line is Jiang's theory of front men. Technologies, billionaires, religions, secret societies, and even public intellectuals need a face that makes hidden power feel personal, cute, rebellious, or divine.

interviews.title: The End Times Are A Geopolitical Machine
interviews.source_title: SNEAKO X Professor Jiang X Aleksandr Dugin | Full Interview
interviews.dek: This is not a neutral theology panel. Sneako brings Jiang and Dugin together because eschatology has become a way to read geopolitics: Russia, Persia, Zionism, American Protestantism, China, finance, Epstein, Trump, and the Middle East all become signs in a fight over the end of history. Jiang supplies the machinery: the Bank of England turns capital into empire, Calvinism turns wealth into chosenness and fear, Christian Zionism makes Zionism into an instrument, and Greater North America becomes America's possible last fortress against Eurasia.

interviews.title: When the Policeman Becomes the Pirate
interviews.source_title: Jiang Xueqin: The Iran War & the Battle for the Petrodollar
interviews.dek: The interview begins with Iran and the petrodollar, but Jiang's answer keeps widening. Hormuz becomes Malacca. Sanctions become forced treasury purchases. Tariffs become naval tolls. Trump becomes less a sovereign actor than the agent a declining empire uses when consent stops working.

interviews.title: Grand Strategy Or Pawnhood
interviews.source_title: Exclusive Interview with Professor Jiang by Dr James Cheong 江学勤独家专访 详细分析美国 伊朗 日本 全世界
interviews.dek: A university lecture becomes a warning to China: tactics, utility, and clever people are not enough. A country without a theory of the world is not a player in history. It is the pawn of those who have one.

interviews.title: The Empire Has No Easy Off-Ramp
interviews.source_title: Jiang Xueqin Finally Breaks His Silence With PBD | PBD #772
interviews.dek: PBD brings Jiang on to challenge the viral Iran prediction. Jiang answers with an empire-decline model: late empires start wars they think will be cheap, then discover that the terrain, the currency system, the oil order, and the enemy’s appetite for sacrifice are stronger than the television script.

interviews.title: When War Becomes a Script and Fame Becomes a Trap
interviews.source_title: SNEAKO interviews Professor Jiang: Global Crisis Incoming
interviews.dek: Sneako presses Jiang after the Iran war turns him into a sudden internet figure. Jiang answers with a warning: war does not merely kill abroad. It scripts civil discord, rewards religious extremism, turns media personalities into controlled assets, and tempts the teacher to become a player in the very game he is trying to explain.

interviews.title: Creativity Is A Scientific Rebellion
interviews.source_title: Professor Jiang on teaching for creativity, agency, and empowerment @PredictiveHistory
interviews.dek: Jiang's education argument begins with a narrow definition and ends with a democratic dream. Creativity is not vibes, artiness, or brainstorming. It is the scientific process inside the classroom: evidence, experiment, mistake, feedback, and the courage to stop obeying authority. That is why Chinese schooling is so hard to change. The obstacle is not only bad pedagogy. It is a whole ecology of teacher prestige, parental fear, test addiction, and top-student narrowness.

interviews.title: Useful Idiot, Predictive History, And The Facts Trap
interviews.source_title: Mehdi Hasan vs. ‘Professor’ Jiang
interviews.dek: Mehdi Hasan does not let Jiang enjoy the Nostradamus frame. He tests the method, the misses, the China question, the Professor persona, the facts-versus-truth distinction, and the language around Jews, Israel, and empire.

interviews.title: Myth Outruns Truth
interviews.source_title: Professor Jiang on His Painful Personal Path | Truth and Myth | A Search for Reality | Internet Fame
interviews.dek: Jay Shapiro does not let Jiang hide inside the viral avatar. He asks who Jiang is, how accurate prediction really is, why secret societies enter the model, whether mythology can outrun truth, and how a person should speak when truth itself may cause harm. Jiang answers with biography, an Asimov-shaped method, a painful Israel trip, eschatology as hidden history, and a defense of open conversation as the only remaining way through the collapse of trust.

interviews.title: The Safe Place Is Not A Place
interviews.source_title: This War Will Not End Quicky | Prof. Jiang Explains
interviews.dek: Kim Iversen brings Jiang on because the channel has become a prediction machine. Jiang answers by defining the machine: history as model testing, game theory as psychology, America as late empire, civil war as elite instrument, Pax Judaica as the next safe house of capital, and survival as a spiritual problem rather than a relocation strategy.

interviews.title: The War Becomes A Rupture
interviews.source_title: Sanchez Effect LIVE - March 26 (w/ Jiang Xueqin)
interviews.dek: Rick Sanchez asks whether a U.S. strike on Iran is about to begin. Jiang's answer turns the weekend scenario into a theory of empire: Trump wants television, Iran can turn either victory or slaughter into attrition, cheap-energy globalization breaks, and rupture becomes the spiritual test hidden inside geopolitical collapse.

interviews.title: The War Climbs Its Own Ladder
interviews.source_title: He Predicted The War in Iran Now Prof. Jiang Predicts This Will Become Trump’s Vietnam | Redacted
interviews.dek: Redacted asks Jiang whether the Iran war is already out of control. Jiang answers with a ladder: official narratives matter less than escalation logic, morale, asymmetric control, elite plans, and the institutional shame that keeps empires gambling after the table has turned against them.

interviews.title: War Gets Its Own Logic
interviews.source_title: Political Prophet Predicts the Next Phase in Iran, Trump’s War Plan, & Israel’s Plot to Sabotage It
interviews.dek: Tucker asks where the Iran war goes next. Jiang answers with a machine of escalation: once war begins, it gains its own momentum, cheap energy breaks, the petrodollar order shakes, Asia remilitarizes, America is tempted into mission creep, Israel is read through Greater Israel and eschatology, and the West ends by forgetting the civilizational books that made it worth defending.

interviews.title: The War Is Looking For A Purpose
interviews.source_title: Prof Jiang: Trump Can't End This War — If He Loses Power, He Goes to Prison
interviews.dek: Tom asks how America can get out of Iran. Jiang answers that the problem is deeper: a declining empire cannot hear bad news, a war without a usable public cause starts searching for strategy, the dollar order needs force where trust has failed, and every exit from Iran threatens to unravel the alliances and capital flows that hold the empire together.

interviews.title: Make The World Safe For AI
interviews.source_title: Professor Jiang - Predictive History | Iran War, Freemasonic Plots, and What's Israel's Problem
interviews.dek: Mark asks how Predictive History began. Jiang answers with a life story that becomes a method: Yale taught him to trust elite order, Afghanistan broke that trust, 2020 and COVID broke it again, and Iran turned his model into a public test. The deepest claim is not that AI is alive. It is that power will try to make people live as if it is.

interviews.title: Attention Is The Real Battleground
interviews.source_title: Jiang Xueqin: Humanity's patterns, the nature of reality, and the battle for your mind.
interviews.dek: Jiang starts with his own formation story: a bullied immigrant reader, Yale disillusionment, depression, poker, game theory, and then a predictive method that treats society as a game played by distinct personalities. But the interview's real center is deeper. Politics was always theater. Plato's cave is not a schoolroom allegory but a live description of rule. Money is only the older god. The real prize is attention, and AI is the candidate replacement deity for a civilization that has forgotten struggle, meaning, and soul.

interviews.title: Our True Wealth Is Consciousness
interviews.source_title: Jiang Xueqin: Our True Wealth Is Our Consciousness | Endgame #259
interviews.dek: Gita asks for Jiang's educational journey. Jiang answers with a life story that becomes a theory of power: poverty taught him hunger, Yale taught him books and hidden hierarchy, journalism taught him truth-seeking and betrayal, AI threatens to capture the attention money once organized, and the only counterpower left is the person's free choice to matter.

interviews.title: War Becomes Its Own Momentum
interviews.source_title: Jiang Xueqin: Iran War Trap Ends U.S. Empire, New World Order is Here
interviews.dek: Danny asks whether Jiang's Iran-war prediction is now playing out. Jiang answers with a chain: America and Iran live in different political realities, Iran can pressure the global economy through the Gulf, retreat would unravel the dollar empire, escalation has its own momentum, and the world after cheap energy will punish societies that still think AI, consumer travel, and imperial money are real wealth.

interviews.title: Empire Breaks When Hormuz Stays Closed
interviews.source_title: ‘Trump Needs to APOLOGIZE!’ Will Iran Claim Victory Over America? | Plus Professor Jiang Interview
interviews.dek: Piers brings Jiang on because two earlier predictions already landed and a third appears to be unfolding: Trump won, war with Iran came, and now the question is whether America can survive the kind of war it started. Jiang's answer is that the real battlefield is not only Iran's military but the economic order that made Gulf glamour, petrodollar finance, cheap energy, AI hype, and American invincibility feel normal.

interviews.title: Hubris, Holy War, and the Petrodollar Trap
interviews.source_title: Jimmy's EXCLUSIVE Interview w/ Professor Xueqin Jiang
interviews.dek: Jimmy Dore brings Jiang on because an earlier prediction seems to have landed: Trump is back, the United States is now at war with Iran, and a forecast once dismissed as wild suddenly looks current. Jiang's answer is that the war only makes sense if America is already deep in imperial decline, if striking Iran can also shatter the petrodollar order, and if some actors are chasing an end-times script rather than a normal strategic victory.

interviews.title: A War Without Purpose Becomes Imperial Suicide
interviews.source_title: Jiang Xueqin: New World Order - Iran War Ends U.S. Empire
interviews.dek: Glenn Diesen asks Jiang the practical questions first: what is this war for, who is exhausting whom, where is the weak point, and why would Washington choose such a disaster? Jiang's answer is that the visible nuclear story is a pretext, the real battlefield is the Gulf energy-finance platform, declining empires start wars they cannot win, and the deepest danger is not miscalculation at the margins but a widening war whose actors still imagine they control the script.

interviews.title: When The Mirage Breaks And War Stops Wanting Victory
interviews.source_title: SNEAKO Interviews Professor Jiang: The End of the World
interviews.dek: Sneako opens by telling Jiang that the predictions have started landing. Jiang answers by saying the war has already escaped normal strategic logic. Israel is stronger than people think, Gulf prosperity is a mirage built on vulnerable infrastructure, the American system is drifting toward draft pressure and domestic fracture, and the deeper struggle now runs through game theory, eschatology, and the fight to keep human beings from becoming obedient components in someone else's holy machine.

interviews.title: Greater Israel Needs America To Bleed In Iran
interviews.source_title: INTERVIEW: Israel will achieve its Greater Israel project
interviews.dek: George Galloway brings Jiang on for an immediate wartime reading, and Jiang answers by turning battlefield questions into a larger trap structure. Israel does not need a clean military win over Iran, he argues. It needs a war that ruins Iran, burns American strength, exposes the GCC's artificial prosperity, ends the cheap-energy world order, and clears the way for a more aggressive Greater Israel project he names Pax Judaica.

interviews.title: The Mirage Breaks, and the Script Turns Eschatological
interviews.source_title: Jiang Xueqin: The Iran War: The Watershed Moment That Changed the Middle East Forever
interviews.dek: The interview opens as a first-week war briefing and then keeps widening. Ground troops become the threshold to World War III. The Gulf becomes a mirage held together by desalination, imported labor, and royal families protected by foreign bases. By the end, Jiang is no longer saying the war is badly explained by ordinary strategy. He is saying ordinary strategy itself no longer explains why the war keeps moving.

interviews.title: Belief Is Reality, and History Follows Sacred Scripts
interviews.source_title: Professor Jiang on The End Times @PredictiveHistory
interviews.dek: The host begins by asking who Jiang is and what Predictive History means. Within minutes Jiang turns history into an AI-style feedback machine, sacred stories into geopolitical scripts, and belief into reality. The interview then moves through Jerusalem, Gog and Magog, Antichrist roles, American civil war, and the social atmosphere that made him want to leave Canada in the first place.

interviews.title: Attrition Breaks The American Aura
interviews.source_title: Professor Jiang Predicts: US WILL LOSE Iran War
interviews.dek: The hosts begin by replaying Jiang's earlier prediction that Trump would win, the United States would fight Iran, and America would lose. Jiang uses the clip as a springboard into a compressed war model: Iran wants attrition, Gulf infrastructure is the soft underbelly of the American economy, cheap drones can puncture imperial prestige, pressure for ground invasion will grow, and the decision for war came from a mix of hubris, patronage, and eschatological script.

interviews.title: War Prediction, Control, And The Rebellion Of Imagination
interviews.source_title: SNEAKO X Professor Jiang: Predicting Iran War
interviews.dek: The title promises Iran war prediction, but the interview's real shape is stranger. Jiang begins by explaining why he predicted conflict with Iran and why listeners project hidden patrons onto him. He then turns that opening into a larger diagnosis: America is split between old finance and new tech oligarchy, Iran would expose the weakness of an optics-first empire, Pax Judaica points toward AI surveillance, and the only durable answer to a control civilization is disciplined attention, imagination, and love.

interviews.title: The Liberal Order Drops The Mask
interviews.source_title: Jiang Xueqin: Great Power Wars Over a New World Order
interviews.dek: Jiang opens by saying the American empire is no longer even pretending to run a liberal order. It now sees allies as vassals, China as a target everywhere, and politics itself as a struggle to replace one elite with another. From there the interview widens: meritocracy is a machine for producing conformists, neoliberalism created the hunger for a strongman, East Asia's success hides deep social collapse, and Iran has finally concluded that Washington and Israel will not stop until war is forced to the end.

interviews.title: The Mafia Empire Meets The Middle Kingdom
interviews.source_title: China will replace US dominance through win-win not warmongering. With Professor Jiang Xuejin
interviews.dek: Jiang opens with the harshest possible premise: empires do not retire peacefully. They flail, overreach, brutalize, and try to convert crisis into emergency rule. From there he widens the frame. The United States now looks less like a liberal hegemon than a mafia empire, Europe looks like a frightened vassal bloc funding corruption through NATO and Ukraine, and Britain's China panic looks absurd beside tolerated American and Israeli spying. Against that, Jiang sketches a different Chinese self-understanding: trade instead of domination, gold-backed discipline instead of dollar corruption, and reciprocity instead of a Faustian will to rule.

interviews.title: Iran Is The Pivot, And Empire Burns The World Around It
interviews.source_title: Global ‘Total War’ Imminent As U.S. Readies Iran Strike | Xueqin Jiang
interviews.dek: Jiang starts from the harshest frame available: Iran is not one more crisis but the hinge on which the next half-century turns. From there the interview sprawls outward in one connected arc. Late America becomes a maritime empire living off chokepoints because softer instruments have failed. Carney's Davos rupture becomes proof that even allies now know the old rules-based order was imperial theater. Canada becomes a toxic asset. Stablecoins become a debt-transfer machine aimed at Chinese savers. And behind all of it sits Jiang's central image of collapse: elites oversell the condemned building, then burn it down.

interviews.title: Iran As Chokepoint, Canada As Toxic Asset
interviews.source_title: Trump's War on Iran / Davos / Canada-China Relations w/ Prof. Jiang Xueqin of Predictive History
interviews.dek: A source-grounded reading of the interview's central move: the Iran file is really about strangling China, while Canada's new China turn is read not as strategy but as a banker trying to offload a toxic asset into Chinese savings.

interviews.title: Empire Runs On Optics, Chokepoints, And Chinese Savings
interviews.source_title: Jiang Xueqin: Predictions for 2026– Trump, Iran & Empire COLLAPSE
interviews.dek: A source-grounded reading of the interview's central move: Iran is treated as the forced war of a declining empire, but the larger target is China, whose trade access, savings, and room to maneuver sit behind almost every theater Jiang discusses.

interviews.title: The Test Is Not The Truth
interviews.source_title: Education in China (Sinica interview with Jiang Xueqin)
interviews.dek: Shanghai can win PISA and still not prove that its schools are forming whole people. Jiang turns the envy around: every test tests test-taking, Gaokao survives because China lacks trust, and the student trained to obey may break when the classroom asks him to speak.

items.title: Attention Is The Real Battleground
items.source_title: Jiang Xueqin: Humanity's patterns, the nature of reality, and the battle for your mind.
items.dek: Jiang starts with his own formation story: a bullied immigrant reader, Yale disillusionment, depression, poker, game theory, and then a predictive method that treats society as a game played by distinct personalities. But the interview's real center is deeper. Politics was always theater. Plato's cave is not a schoolroom allegory but a live description of rule. Money is only the older god. The real prize is attention, and AI is the candidate replacement deity for a civilization that has forgotten struggle, meaning, and soul.

items.title: Our True Wealth Is Consciousness
items.source_title: Jiang Xueqin: Our True Wealth Is Our Consciousness | Endgame #259
items.dek: Gita asks for Jiang's educational journey. Jiang answers with a life story that becomes a theory of power: poverty taught him hunger, Yale taught him books and hidden hierarchy, journalism taught him truth-seeking and betrayal, AI threatens to capture the attention money once organized, and the only counterpower left is the person's free choice to matter.

items.title: War Becomes Its Own Momentum
items.source_title: Jiang Xueqin: Iran War Trap Ends U.S. Empire, New World Order is Here
items.dek: Danny asks whether Jiang's Iran-war prediction is now playing out. Jiang answers with a chain: America and Iran live in different political realities, Iran can pressure the global economy through the Gulf, retreat would unravel the dollar empire, escalation has its own momentum, and the world after cheap energy will punish societies that still think AI, consumer travel, and imperial money are real wealth.

items.title: Empire Breaks When Hormuz Stays Closed
items.source_title: ‘Trump Needs to APOLOGIZE!’ Will Iran Claim Victory Over America? | Plus Professor Jiang Interview
items.dek: Piers brings Jiang on because two earlier predictions already landed and a third appears to be unfolding: Trump won, war with Iran came, and now the question is whether America can survive the kind of war it started. Jiang's answer is that the real battlefield is not only Iran's military but the economic order that made Gulf glamour, petrodollar finance, cheap energy, AI hype, and American invincibility feel normal.

items.title: Hubris, Holy War, and the Petrodollar Trap
items.source_title: Jimmy's EXCLUSIVE Interview w/ Professor Xueqin Jiang
items.dek: Jimmy Dore brings Jiang on because an earlier prediction seems to have landed: Trump is back, the United States is now at war with Iran, and a forecast once dismissed as wild suddenly looks current. Jiang's answer is that the war only makes sense if America is already deep in imperial decline, if striking Iran can also shatter the petrodollar order, and if some actors are chasing an end-times script rather than a normal strategic victory.

items.title: A War Without Purpose Becomes Imperial Suicide
items.source_title: Jiang Xueqin: New World Order - Iran War Ends U.S. Empire
items.dek: Glenn Diesen asks Jiang the practical questions first: what is this war for, who is exhausting whom, where is the weak point, and why would Washington choose such a disaster? Jiang's answer is that the visible nuclear story is a pretext, the real battlefield is the Gulf energy-finance platform, declining empires start wars they cannot win, and the deepest danger is not miscalculation at the margins but a widening war whose actors still imagine they control the script.

items.title: When The Mirage Breaks And War Stops Wanting Victory
items.source_title: SNEAKO Interviews Professor Jiang: The End of the World
items.dek: Sneako opens by telling Jiang that the predictions have started landing. Jiang answers by saying the war has already escaped normal strategic logic. Israel is stronger than people think, Gulf prosperity is a mirage built on vulnerable infrastructure, the American system is drifting toward draft pressure and domestic fracture, and the deeper struggle now runs through game theory, eschatology, and the fight to keep human beings from becoming obedient components in someone else's holy machine.

items.title: Greater Israel Needs America To Bleed In Iran
items.source_title: INTERVIEW: Israel will achieve its Greater Israel project
items.dek: George Galloway brings Jiang on for an immediate wartime reading, and Jiang answers by turning battlefield questions into a larger trap structure. Israel does not need a clean military win over Iran, he argues. It needs a war that ruins Iran, burns American strength, exposes the GCC's artificial prosperity, ends the cheap-energy world order, and clears the way for a more aggressive Greater Israel project he names Pax Judaica.

items.title: The Mirage Breaks, and the Script Turns Eschatological
items.source_title: Jiang Xueqin: The Iran War: The Watershed Moment That Changed the Middle East Forever
items.dek: The interview opens as a first-week war briefing and then keeps widening. Ground troops become the threshold to World War III. The Gulf becomes a mirage held together by desalination, imported labor, and royal families protected by foreign bases. By the end, Jiang is no longer saying the war is badly explained by ordinary strategy. He is saying ordinary strategy itself no longer explains why the war keeps moving.

items.title: Belief Is Reality, and History Follows Sacred Scripts
items.source_title: Professor Jiang on The End Times @PredictiveHistory
items.dek: The host begins by asking who Jiang is and what Predictive History means. Within minutes Jiang turns history into an AI-style feedback machine, sacred stories into geopolitical scripts, and belief into reality. The interview then moves through Jerusalem, Gog and Magog, Antichrist roles, American civil war, and the social atmosphere that made him want to leave Canada in the first place.

items.title: Attrition Breaks The American Aura
items.source_title: Professor Jiang Predicts: US WILL LOSE Iran War
items.dek: The hosts begin by replaying Jiang's earlier prediction that Trump would win, the United States would fight Iran, and America would lose. Jiang uses the clip as a springboard into a compressed war model: Iran wants attrition, Gulf infrastructure is the soft underbelly of the American economy, cheap drones can puncture imperial prestige, pressure for ground invasion will grow, and the decision for war came from a mix of hubris, patronage, and eschatological script.

items.title: War Prediction, Control, And The Rebellion Of Imagination
items.source_title: SNEAKO X Professor Jiang: Predicting Iran War
items.dek: The title promises Iran war prediction, but the interview's real shape is stranger. Jiang begins by explaining why he predicted conflict with Iran and why listeners project hidden patrons onto him. He then turns that opening into a larger diagnosis: America is split between old finance and new tech oligarchy, Iran would expose the weakness of an optics-first empire, Pax Judaica points toward AI surveillance, and the only durable answer to a control civilization is disciplined attention, imagination, and love.

items.title: The Liberal Order Drops The Mask
items.source_title: Jiang Xueqin: Great Power Wars Over a New World Order
items.dek: Jiang opens by saying the American empire is no longer even pretending to run a liberal order. It now sees allies as vassals, China as a target everywhere, and politics itself as a struggle to replace one elite with another. From there the interview widens: meritocracy is a machine for producing conformists, neoliberalism created the hunger for a strongman, East Asia's success hides deep social collapse, and Iran has finally concluded that Washington and Israel will not stop until war is forced to the end.

items.title: The Mafia Empire Meets The Middle Kingdom
items.source_title: China will replace US dominance through win-win not warmongering. With Professor Jiang Xuejin
items.dek: Jiang opens with the harshest possible premise: empires do not retire peacefully. They flail, overreach, brutalize, and try to convert crisis into emergency rule. From there he widens the frame. The United States now looks less like a liberal hegemon than a mafia empire, Europe looks like a frightened vassal bloc funding corruption through NATO and Ukraine, and Britain's China panic looks absurd beside tolerated American and Israeli spying. Against that, Jiang sketches a different Chinese self-understanding: trade instead of domination, gold-backed discipline instead of dollar corruption, and reciprocity instead of a Faustian will to rule.

items.title: Iran Is The Pivot, And Empire Burns The World Around It
items.source_title: Global ‘Total War’ Imminent As U.S. Readies Iran Strike | Xueqin Jiang
items.dek: Jiang starts from the harshest frame available: Iran is not one more crisis but the hinge on which the next half-century turns. From there the interview sprawls outward in one connected arc. Late America becomes a maritime empire living off chokepoints because softer instruments have failed. Carney's Davos rupture becomes proof that even allies now know the old rules-based order was imperial theater. Canada becomes a toxic asset. Stablecoins become a debt-transfer machine aimed at Chinese savers. And behind all of it sits Jiang's central image of collapse: elites oversell the condemned building, then burn it down.

items.title: Iran As Chokepoint, Canada As Toxic Asset
items.source_title: Trump's War on Iran / Davos / Canada-China Relations w/ Prof. Jiang Xueqin of Predictive History
items.dek: A source-grounded reading of the interview's central move: the Iran file is really about strangling China, while Canada's new China turn is read not as strategy but as a banker trying to offload a toxic asset into Chinese savings.

items.title: Empire Runs On Optics, Chokepoints, And Chinese Savings
items.source_title: Jiang Xueqin: Predictions for 2026– Trump, Iran & Empire COLLAPSE
items.dek: A source-grounded reading of the interview's central move: Iran is treated as the forced war of a declining empire, but the larger target is China, whose trade access, savings, and room to maneuver sit behind almost every theater Jiang discusses.

items.title: The Test Is Not The Truth
items.source_title: Education in China (Sinica interview with Jiang Xueqin)
items.dek: Shanghai can win PISA and still not prove that its schools are forming whole people. Jiang turns the envy around: every test tests test-taking, Gaokao survives because China lacks trust, and the student trained to obey may break when the classroom asks him to speak.
